executive_summary.task: Analyze Mk VI telemetry implementation to identify features for Mk VII port
executive_summary.key_finding: Mk VI telemetry provided excellent observability but was overly complex (30+ column CSV, 700+ LOC). Mk VII has a solid simplified foundation (11 columns, ~350 LOC) but lacks critical queue monitoring and real-time metrics integration.
executive_summary.recommendation: Port 4 high-priority features from Mk VI: queue overflow tracking, per-module event logging, SharedMetrics integration, and detailed latency breakdown. Skip thread affinity tracking and hardware-specific fields (buffer_fd, PWM values) as they add complexity without proportional value.

unified_csv_format.description: Wide row format with 30+ columns capturing all pipeline stages in single row

timestamp_strategy.produced_ts_epoch_ms: System clock epoch milliseconds for absolute wall-clock correlation
timestamp_strategy.monotonic_ms: Steady clock relative to session start for precise latency deltas

run_json_structure.notes: Mk VI run.json was minimal; Mk VII expanded this significantly with statistics

session_management.rotation_policy: Keep 3 latest sessions, archive older to tar.gz

per_frame_accounting.description: Every frame logged with complete pipeline timing from capture to actuation
per_frame_accounting.value: Enabled precise latency attribution (camera vs ISP vs inference vs decision)
per_frame_accounting.implementation: TelemetryEntry struct with 35+ fields, logged via log_full_entry()

queue_monitoring.description: Track queue depth before/after processing, count overflows
queue_monitoring.value: Critical for identifying backpressure and bottleneck stages

thread_affinity_tracking.description: Log which CPU core and priority each thread ran on
thread_affinity_tracking.value: Helped debug scheduling issues and verify real-time pinning
thread_affinity_tracking.implementation: thread_id, thread_cpu, thread_priority columns populated from pthread_getschedparam()

shared_metrics_integration.description: Telemetry updated SharedMetrics struct for real-time TUI display
shared_metrics_integration.value: Enabled live monitoring dashboard without parsing CSV
shared_metrics_integration.implementation: monitor::SharedMetrics with atomics for CPU, memory, queue depths, latency

hardware_failure_handling.description: Log hardware failures with graceful degradation paths
hardware_failure_handling.value: Enabled debugging of production issues where camera/TPU failed
hardware_failure_handling.implementation: log_event() calls for camera_failure, tpu_unavailable, pwm_device_unavailable

safety_invariant_monitoring.description: Log safety violations and gate actuation on thermal/CPU thresholds
safety_invariant_monitoring.value: Critical for safety certification and debugging false positives
safety_invariant_monitoring.implementation: log_safety_invariant_violation() updates both CSV and SharedMetrics

what_worked_well: Unified CSV format enabled easy post-hoc analysis with standard tools (awk, pandas)
Queue overflow tracking identified bottlenecks in inference pipeline
SharedMetrics allowed real-time TUI monitoring without CSV parsing overhead
Session-based organization with automatic rotation kept disk usage bounded
Per-frame latency breakdown helped optimize hot paths (achieved <15ms end-to-end)
what_was_overly_complex: 30+ column CSV was hard to maintain - adding fields required header changes
FixedString templates added 100+ LOC for marginal allocation savings
Thread affinity tracking was Pi-specific and added platform complexity
Hardware-specific fields (cam_buffer_fd, servo_pwm_value) not useful for debugging
Dual timestamp strategy (epoch + monotonic) confused some analysis scripts
Bootstrap buffer logic for early-init logs was fragile and hard to test

csv_format.timestamp_strategy: Single CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW nanoseconds (simpler than Mk VI dual approach)

fields: latency_statistics (vision, track, actuation with avg_ns, max_ns, avg_ms)
run_json_structure.assessment: Well-designed, more comprehensive than Mk VI

dependencies: Standard library only (no nlohmann/json - manual JSON writing)

design_choices.inline_statistics: update_statistics() called inline in log_frame() for performance
design_choices.manual_json: Avoids nlohmann/json dependency, writes JSON manually

missing_from_mk_vi: No queue overflow tracking (only current depth)
No SharedMetrics integration for real-time monitoring
improvements_over_mk_vi: Simpler CSV format (11 vs 30+ columns) - easier to maintain

feature_recommendations.rationale: Critical for identifying backpressure. Mk VI showed this was essential for debugging pipeline stalls.
feature_recommendations.implementation: Add queue_overflow_count to TelemetryFrame, increment in camera/encoder when dropping frames
code_changes: Add uint16_t queue_overflow_count to TelemetryFrame struct
Add to CSV header and flush_buffer() output
Add to SessionMetadata statistics
Update run.json schema

feature_recommendations.rationale: Mk VI's log_event() was invaluable for logging non-frame events (hardware failures, state transitions, safety violations).
feature_recommendations.implementation: Add log_event(module, event, notes) method that writes CSV row with minimal fields
code_changes: Add log_event(uint64_t timestamp_ns, const char* module, const char* event, const char* notes) method
Event rows use special frame_id=UINT64_MAX to distinguish from frame rows
Update CSV parser to handle sparse event rows

feature_recommendations.rationale: Enables real-time TUI/dashboard without CSV parsing. Mk VI used this for live monitoring.
feature_recommendations.implementation: Create monitor::SharedMetrics struct with atomics, pass pointer to TelemetryWriter
code_changes: Create include/aurore/monitor/shared_metrics.hpp
Add SharedMetrics* to TelemetryWriter constructor
Update atomics in log_frame() (cpu_percent, queue_depth, latencies)
Add safety_violation_reason string (mutex-protected)

feature_recommendations.rationale: Mk VI's camera_failure, tpu_unavailable events were critical for production debugging.
code_changes: Add log_hardware_event(const char* device, const char* error, const char* fallback_action)
Pre-defined event strings for common failures
Log to both CSV and SharedMetrics

feature_recommendations.rationale: Mk VI tracked inference_start/end separately. Mk VII could benefit from finer granularity.
feature_recommendations.implementation: Add inference_latency_ns, decision_latency_ns separate from vision/track/actuation
code_changes: Add inference_latency_ns, decision_latency_ns to TelemetryFrame
Update CSV header and statistics calculation
Update run.json latency_statistics section

feature_recommendations.rationale: Mk VI tracked thread_cpu/priority but this was Pi-specific and added complexity. Skip for Mk VII's simplified design.
feature_recommendations.recommendation: SKIP - not porting. Deterministic scheduling is handled by systemd/OS config, not telemetry.

feature_recommendations.rationale: Mk VI tracked cam_buffer_fd, servo_pwm_value but these were rarely useful for debugging.
feature_recommendations.recommendation: SKIP - not porting. Add to notes field if specific debugging needed.

feature_recommendations.rationale: Mk VI used both epoch and monotonic timestamps. Mk VII's single monotonic approach is simpler and sufficient.
feature_recommendations.recommendation: SKIP - keep Mk VII's simpler single-timestamp design

feature_recommendations.rationale: Mk VI archived old sessions to tar.gz. Mk VII just deletes. Archiving is useful for long-term analysis.
feature_recommendations.implementation: Add archive_dir config option, compress old sessions with libz
code_changes: Add archive_dir to TelemetryConfig
Add compress_session() method using popen() with tar/gzip
Update rotate_logs() to archive instead of delete

next_steps.details: Add queue_overflow_count field to TelemetryFrame and CSV output
Implement log_event() method for non-frame events
Create shared_metrics.hpp and integrate with TelemetryWriter
Implement convenience methods for common hardware failures
Update telemetry_writer.hpp documentation with new features
Test new event logging, overflow tracking, SharedMetrics updates

risk_assessment.rationale: All features are straightforward additions to existing telemetry infrastructure. No architectural changes required.
risk_assessment.dependencies: None - all features use standard library
risk_assessment.compatibility: CSV format changes are additive (new columns), existing parsers will ignore new fields
